title: daucus carota subsp. boissieri
intro: Daucus carota subsp. boissieri, commonly known as wild carrot, is a subspecies of the wild carrot plant that grows in the wild, typically found in moist areas with rich soil. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation in the body. The health benefits of Daucus carota subsp. boissieri include its potential to aid in digestive issues, lower cholesterol levels, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote overall health and well-being.
